scope: ExecPolicy DSL parsing, rule evaluation, amendments, and checker JSON

requiredBehaviors: parse allow, prompt, and forbidden decisions
match prefix rules against command token prefixes
expand alternatives in the first pattern token into distinct rules
keep tail alternatives grouped within one rule
choose the strictest decision across all matching rules
edgeCases: empty prefixes are rejected before insertion
commands with no matching rule use the supplied heuristic decision
multiple command evaluations aggregate all matched rules before choosing a decision
matching rule output keeps matched prefixes and justifications

requiredBehaviors: parse prefix_rule, network_rule, and host_executable calls
parse strings, nested lists, comments, whitespace, and trailing commas

parse top-level assignments, identifier references, string/list + expressions, parenthesized expressions, helper functions with return expressions, for loops over lists, list comprehensions, positional arguments, and f-string substitutions from string variables
validate match and not_match examples at parse time
carry source locations on parse and validation failures
edgeCases: negative examples fail when any rule matches them
the carried example corpus parses and evaluates representative commands

requiredBehaviors: normalize network rule hosts and protocol aliases
compile allowed and denied network domains with last writer wins
record host executable allowlists by executable basename
resolve absolute executable paths to basename prefix rules when enabled
edgeCases: host and Windows executable lookup lowercasing is ASCII-only
bracketed IPv6 hosts with ports normalize to the literal
explicit empty host executable allowlists block basename fallback
exact absolute-path prefix rules take precedence over host executable resolution

requiredBehaviors: append normalized network rules with policy-file locking
load multiple policy files through the parser
render checker JSON with external-tagged rule matches
edgeCases: missing final newlines are inserted before appending
invalid network amendment hosts are rejected before writing
checker argv parsing requires at least one rule file and command tokens
